description: Implement a greenfield Python CLI tool called `greet` that displays stylized 'Hello, World!' greetings in 10 human languages (English, French, Spanish, German, Japanese, Mandarin, Arabic, Hindi, Swahili, Portuguese) with pyfiglet ASCII art banners. The tool uses Click for CLI parsing, Rich for terminal formatting, and pyfiglet for figlet banners, and supports 12 display options: language filtering (--languages/-l), personalization (--name), display toggles (--no-figlet, --no-color, --random, --box), fun modes (--cowsay, --party), fortune quotes (--fortune), grid layout (--all-at-once), and animation effects (--typewriter, --rainbow). All language and proverb data must be data-driven via typed dataclasses, so adding a new language requires only a single data entry with zero logic changes. The implementation must pass all four quality gates: pytest, mypy --strict src/, ruff check, and ruff format --check.
title: greet-cli
